## Command

```powershell
cmake --build C:\dev\public\engine-revival-workspaces\brender-v132-portable-core-build-period-defines-2026-07-03 --target brender_core_float
```

## Result Summary

The updated harness configured and began compiling with MSVC after adding FLOAT, fixed-inline-disabled, and period release build definitions. The build then failed because the harness source glob included files that the period makefiles did not compile as normal C translation units.

## Artifacts Policy

The materialized harness, configure tree, and compiler transcript were kept outside this metadata repo. No generated binaries, copied source, private assets, or restricted SDK material are committed.

## Public Notes

This attempt verifies that the portable harness moved past the previous mscifix.h inline assembly and undefined STATIC blockers. It does not claim a completed library build.

## Blockers

- portable harness still globbed all C files instead of using period makefile object lists
- core/fw/foo.c is present in the source checkout but is not part of the period fw OBJS_C list
- core/fw/pretok.c is token-table data that needs the period token-generation boundary reconstructed before direct compilation

## Next Actions

- replace module-wide C file globbing with explicit source lists derived from period makefiles
- model the fw pretok token-generation boundary before compiling framework sources
- rerun brender_core_float after the source-selection harness is materialized
